test.notes: 1029 tests in 122 suites passed in 43.9 seconds

typecheck.command: xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-macOS -configuration Debug build && xcodebuild -scheme NetMonitor-iOS -configuration Debug build
typecheck.notes: Both macOS and iOS targets BUILD SUCCEEDED under SWIFT_STRICT_CONCURRENCY: complete

lint.notes: SwiftLint: 0 errors (warnings only, all pre-existing). SwiftFormat: 0 files require formatting.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated architecture.md: WiFiman color scheme now supports per-visualization normalization ranges (not signal-strength-only as previously documented). Added linkSpeed data gap note. Added ViewModel size guidance. Added Logger.heatmap usage note.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for platform-only protocol placement: 'Service protocols that are specific to a single platform (e.g., CoreWLANServiceProtocol for macOS) may be defined in the platform target's Platform/ directory rather than in ServiceProtocols.swift. Cross-platform protocols must remain in ServiceProtocols.swift.' This resolves the ambiguity that led a worker to define CoreWLANServiceProtocol locally.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: macos-canvas-survey-workflow worker defined CoreWLANServiceProtocol in NetMonitor-macOS/Platform/ instead of ServiceProtocols.swift. The AGENTS.md says 'Service protocols: *Protocol suffix in ServiceProtocols.swift' but doesn't address platform-specific protocols.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for stub/no-op service placement: 'No-op service implementations (e.g., NoOpSpeedTestService) should be placed in the Platform/ directory of their target, not inside View files.' Two workers placed NoOpSpeedTestService inside HeatmapProjectListView.swift.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: macos-canvas-survey-workflow and macos-save-load-export both have NoOpSpeedTestService defined in HeatmapProjectListView.swift (a View file). Convention says services go in Platform/.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for shared test helpers: 'When the same test utility function is needed across multiple test files, extract it to a shared TestHelpers.swift file in the test target. Avoid duplicating test helper functions verbatim across files.' Three test files contain identical makeTestPNGData() and makeTestPNGFile() implementations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: HeatmapSurveyViewModelTests.swift, HeatmapSaveLoadExportTests.swift, and HeatmapActiveScanUndoTests.swift all contain identical 20-line implementations of makeTestPNGData() and makeTestPNGFile().

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention for enum display names: 'Enums defined in NetMonitorCore that need human-readable display names should have a public computed property (e.g., `var displayName: String`) on the enum itself, not duplicated in ViewModels and Views.' This avoids the pattern of duplicating switch statements.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: macos-heatmap-overlay-visualizations has identical displayName switch statements in both HeatmapSurveyViewModel (line 162) and HeatmapSurveyView (line 329) for HeatmapVisualization cases.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update macos-ui-worker and swift-core-worker skills: (1) Note that TDD red phase is optional for placeholder features with no business logic, AppKit-heavy features, and features extending existing ViewModels. (2) Add rsync fallback instruction for mac-mini sync: 'If mac-mini git pull fails due to credential error, use: rsync -avz --exclude .git --exclude DerivedData . mac-mini:~/Projects/NetMonitor-2.0/'. (3) Clarify that interactive verification (Step 6) may be skipped in headless environments.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: 6 of 7 features deviated from TDD red phase with reasonable justifications. 3 features used rsync as fallback for mac-mini sync. 2 features skipped interactive verification. All produced quality output despite deviations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add ViewModel size guidance: 'When a ViewModel approaches the SwiftLint file_length warning (600 lines), split functionality into focused extensions in separate files (e.g., HeatmapSurveyViewModel+Undo.swift, HeatmapSurveyViewModel+Export.swift). Each extension file should use MARK sections and handle one area of concern.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: HeatmapSurveyViewModel.swift is at 954 lines (633 excluding comments/whitespace per SwiftLint warning). Multiple workers noted it's growing large. The file already triggers file_length warning.

rejectedObservations.observation: Logger.heatmap category is defined but Logger.app is used in heatmap code
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a non-blocking code issue (use correct logger category), not a shared state gap. Workers should use Logger.heatmap for heatmap features — this is already implied by the existing logging convention. No new AGENTS.md rule needed.

The library directory exists and contains architecture.md, environment.md, and user-testing.md. The skill's references are valid. The observation from the floor-plan-import worker may have been from an early state before the directory was populated.

rejectedObservations.observation: user-testing.md not updated with new testing surfaces (PDF export, draw tool, etc.)
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a worker oversight on a specific file, not a systemic shared state issue. The user-testing-validator phase will handle this naturally as it identifies testable surfaces.

rejectedObservations.observation: Calibration marker geometry doesn't account for aspect-fit padding
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a non-blocking code bug in a specific view, not a shared state observation. It's correctly noted in the feature review but doesn't warrant a conventions or library change.

rejectedObservations.observation: WiFiInfo model lacks linkSpeed field causing nil in production
rejectedObservations.reason: Applied to architecture.md as a known data gap note instead. This is a factual codebase observation appropriate for library, not an AGENTS.md convention.
